heading: Grants
label: 300ff–21 Grants
text: The Secretary shall, subject to the availability of appropriations, make grants to States to enable such States to improve the quality, availability and organization of health care and support services for individuals and families with HIV/AIDS. The authority of the Secretary to provide grants under this section is subject to section 300ff–34(e)(2) of this title (relating to the decrease in perinatal transmission of HIV/AIDS).1See References in Text note below.1

L. 111–87, § 2(a)(1)Oct. 1, 2009Sept. 30, 2009, substituted “HIV/AIDS” for “HIV disease” in two places.Pub. L. 109–415, § 702(3), substituted “this section” for “this part”.Pub. L. 109–415, § 204(a), struck out subsec. (a) designation and heading before “The Secretary” and struck out subsec. (b) which related to priority for women, infants, and children.Pub. L. 109–415, § 201(c)(1)2000—Subsec. (b). amended heading and text of subsec. (b) generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “For the purpose of providing health and support services to infants, children, and women with HIV disease, including treatment measures to prevent the perinatal transmission of HIV, a State shall use, of the funds allocated under this part to the State for a fiscal year, not less than the percentage constituted by the ratio of the population in the State of infants, children, and women with acquired immune deficiency syndrome to the general population in the State of individuals with such syndrome.”Pub. (a). , inserted at end “The authority of the Secretary to provide grants under this part is subject to (relating to the decrease in perinatal transmission of HIV disease).”Pub.
